pages.raw_text:
Another opportunity will be given
for the city council to again 90 on
record on the question of the pay OF
men and teams employed on city
work at the meeting of the city
council next Tuesday night.


Councilman Lambert states that
he will call up his compromise
measure giving teams Sc per day,
instead Of $3.50, as originally asked,
and will urge that it be passed.


Councilman Funk, the father of
the SO day wage for day laborers,
who introduced the matter origin
ally in the city council, will again
revive his ordinance and urge its
reconsideration and passage.


Councilmen have heard of the
storm brewing among the forces of
organized labor on the question of
a wage scale and may proceed to
reconsider and enact the increase
in wages without much pressure.


F. G. Robertson, the attorney, has
consented to appear and address
the council on behalf of the work
ingmen. d. G. Coates, chairman of
the committee from the Central
Labor council that is planning the
protest. will address the council in
favor of the raise and for Jail mat
rons, both of which propositions are
united in the effort being made by
the Central Labor organization.
